menu_options.person: Extend a person (attach states to an existing person)
menu_options.custom: Create a custom state (a new statecraft.* entity)

person.title: Extend a person
person.description: Pick the person these states attach to. You'll define the states themselves in the Statecraft panel.

custom.title: Create a custom state
custom.description: Creates a new entity, for example statecraft.house_state, whose state you define from conditions in the Statecraft panel. The default state is used when no state matches.

init.title: Statecraft
init.description: States are evaluated top to bottom; the first one that matches wins.

state_meta.title: State
state_meta.description: Name the state and choose how to author its enter condition. The optional hold condition keeps the state active once entered, while it stays true, even after the enter condition goes false (for example: stay 'sleep' until the door opens). Leave it blank for none.

state_yaml.title: Condition (YAML)
state_yaml.description: A native Home Assistant condition: a single condition, or an and/or/not block.

source_form.title: Source
source_form.description: A single check. State match: the entity is one of the listed states. Numeric threshold: the entity's value is above and/or below a bound. 'For' requires it to hold that long.

data.negate: Invert (entity is NOT one of these)

error.invalid_condition: That is not a valid Home Assistant condition.
error.source_entity: Pick an entity for this source.
error.source_states: Add at least one match state, or switch to a numeric threshold.
error.source_bounds: Set an above and/or below value for a numeric threshold.
error.source_time: Set an after and/or before time for a time source.

person_patch_disabled.title: Statecraft can't attach to the person component
person_patch_disabled.description: Statecraft layers composite states onto your person entities by wrapping the core person component (built against {built_against}). That wrapping failed to install, so person scopes are showing plain presence (home / not_home) instead of your composite states. This usually means a Home Assistant update changed the person component. Check for a Statecraft update, then restart Home Assistant. Custom scopes are unaffected.

set_override.name: Set override
set_override.description: Pin a scope to a state, bypassing its conditions, then revert to automatic after an optional duration.

entity_id.name: Scope
entity_id.description: The person or statecraft entity to override.

state.name: State
state.description: The state to force (e.g. dnd, party, sleep).

duration.name: Duration
duration.description: How long to hold the override before reverting to automatic. Leave empty to hold until cleared.

clear_override.name: Clear override
clear_override.description: Drop a manual override and return the scope to automatic evaluation.

entity_id.name: Scope
entity_id.description: The person or statecraft entity to return to automatic.
